title: Intraperitoneal Glucose Tolerance Test in Mice
author: Henri Brunengraber
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ydtfs6n

steps.title: Fast mice overnight or for 6 hours
steps.instruction: Fast mice overnight or for 6 hours. Remove mouse from cage and put into a clean cage with water and no food (5:00PM). Next day begin GTT by 9:00AM.

steps.title: Weigh each mouse and record weight
steps.instruction: Weigh each mouse and record weight.

steps.title: Insert glucose strip into glucometer and...
steps.instruction: Insert glucose strip into glucometer and check that the code matches for the strip being used.

steps.title: Take fasting blood glucose by snipping...
steps.instruction: Take fasting blood glucose by snipping the tail and putting a drop on the glucose strip (already in glucometer). Record fasting glucose as time 0’. Dip tail into clotisol to clot blood.

steps.title: Calculate the amount of glucose needed...
steps.instruction: Calculate the amount of glucose needed for a concentration of 2gm glucose/kg body weight. Record the volume for each mouse.

steps.title: Inject each mouse 3’ apart for time 0
steps.instruction: Inject each mouse 3’ apart for time 0. Repeat #4 at 15min, 30 min, 60 min and 120 min after IP injection of glucose.

steps.title: Do not take extra blood for insulin measurements
steps.instruction: Do not take extra blood for insulin measurements. This causes too much stress. Use a separate group of mice for collecting blood after glucose injection for insulin measurements.
